info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Subscriber Management API
info.description: The Subscriber Onboarding API facilitates the registration and management of new subscribers within the system. This endpoint allows businesses to capture essential subscriber information and streamline the onboarding process. Typical use cases include service providers looking to expand their customer base by integrating seamless subscriber management into their platforms. The API ensures a smooth flow of data, enhancing the user experience and operational efficiency.

post.summary: Create a new subscriber for a customer.
post.description: This operation allows you to create a new subscriber under a specified customer. Provide the necessary subscriber details in the request body to successfully add them to the subscriber list.

requestBody.description: The request body for subscribing a new customer. It contains the details needed to create a subscription for a customer. Ensure that all required fields are present and formatted correctly.

500.description: An unexpected server error occurred while processing the request.

200.description: The request was successful and the subscriber information is returned.

404.description: The requested subscriber resource could not be found.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: Error Response for Subscriber Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates if the operation was successful or not.

error.description: A short description of the error that occurred.

message.description: A descriptive message explaining the error.
message.example: Unable to process subscriber activation request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The endpoint path where the error occurred.

trace.description: A stack trace or error details for debugging purposes.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for the user on how to proceed after the error.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: This schema represents the structure of the error response returned when there is a server error during the subscriber operations.

example.message: Unable to process subscriber activation request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database connection timeout while saving subscriber details.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

customerId.description: The ID of the customer associated with the error.

customerType.description: The type of the customer (e.g., individual, business).

errorCode.description: A specific error code representing the error.

message.description: A message detailing the nature of the error.
message.example: Customer with the specified ID does not exist.

region.description: The geographical region associated with the customer.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: Detailed information about the error related to the customer.

example.message: Customer with the specified ID does not exist.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: Subscriber Response

subscriberId.description: The unique identifier for the subscriber.

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer associated with the subscriber.

msisdn.description: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) of the subscriber.

plan.description: The subscription plan assigned to the subscriber.

network.description: The network provider for the subscriber.

status.description: Current status of the subscriber's account.

deviceType.description: Type of device used by the subscriber.

simType.description: Type of SIM card used by the subscriber.

activatedAt.description: Date and time when the subscriber's account was activated.

message.description: A message providing additional information about the subscriber.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: Schema representing the response for a successful subscriber creation operation.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: Subscriber Creation Request

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

msisdn.description: The Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) for the subscriber.

firstName.description: The first name of the subscriber.

lastName.description: The last name of the subscriber.

plan.description: The plan associated with the subscriber.

network.description: The network provider for the subscriber.

activationDate.description: The date when the subscription becomes active.

deviceType.description: The type of device used by the subscriber.

simType.description: The type of SIM card used by the subscriber.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: This schema represents the request body used for creating a new subscriber in the system.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the request was successful or not.

error.description: A brief description of the error that occurred.

message.example: Customer details not found for subscriber activation.

path.description: The path of the request that resulted in the error.

trace.description: A trace or stack trace for debugging the error.

details.description: Additional details related to the error.

suggestion.description: Suggestions on how to resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Verify customer information and retry the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: Response schema for a 404 error when attempting to subscribe a customer.

example.message: Customer details not found for subscriber activation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer information and retry the request.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates the caching behavior by specifying the headers that may vary in the response.

Location.description: Provides the URL to the resource that might be created or referenced, typically used in responses that suggest a new location based on the action taken.

content-type.description: Specifies the media type of the resource returned in the response, aiding in content negotiation.
